title: Uniface Messages 0000-0999
text: Uniface Messages 0000-0999 Refer to the Uniface message number, message text, and an explanation of the message. The following table refers to messages with numbering between 0000 - 0999 . Search: Column Message number Message text Explanation Filter results... 158 rows found Message number Message text Explanation Filter 158 rows found Filter 158 rows found Filter 158 rows found Message number Message text Explanation 0001 Form services requested before form is loaded. Uniface component services were requested from a 3GL program before a component was loaded. Ensure that the component is loaded before a component service is requested. 0006 Error detected in the apStart trigger Check the ProcScript in the apStart trigger of the application shell. Check that all components specified in the activate or run statements are available. If $status contains a negative value after the trigger has completed, an error is present in the assignment file. 0007 No application table found. The application table was not found. Check your system configuration and recompile your components and application. If the error occurs again, contact your Uniface representative. 0008 Failed to activate Application shell screen. Uniface cannot locate the .aps file that contains the application screen definition. Ensure that your application compiles successfully. If the error occurs again, contact your system administrator. 0009 No FORMAREA available for component. Uniface cannot find a FORMAREA on which to map components. Check the application shell Paint Tableau and ensure that the FORMAREA field frame is defined. Recompile your components and application. If the error occurs again, contact your system administrator. 0010 No Procs defined in apStart trigger This trigger contains no ProcScript statements. It should contain at least an activate statement to start the first component. 0011 Error initializing specified keyboard. Uniface cannot load a keyboard translation table for the keyboard layout requested. Ensure that the ProcScript statement or assignment, or both, are correct and that the library containing the table you want is available. 0013 Cannot reopen form file *.FRM to save prototype data. Uniface cannot open the .frm file to store prototype data. Check your system for hardware problems or inappropriate file protection. The component may already be in use; wait a moment and try to accept the prototype data again. 0016 Terminal input aborted; not allowed in batch mode. While operating in batch mode, Uniface tried to execute a ProcScript statement that requires input from the user, such as edit , display , askmess , and so on. This is not allowed. Avoid using a ProcScript statement that requires input from the terminal, or test for $batch before issuing such statements. 0017 Incomplete logon information. An application that was started in batch mode (using the /bat switch) attempted to access data from a DBMS, but did not have sufficient logon information. You can restart the application using the /log switch to provide logon information, or specify the complete logon in your assignment file. 0019 Form FormName version is incorrect; you must recompile it. A run statement tried to activate a form that was not compiled with the current version of Uniface. Compile the form or forms. 0020 File FileName not recognized as application or component. Uniface performs a simple check to recognize component and application files. This message is generated if the 4GL run statement contains a reference to a file that is not a component or an application. Check the file you want to run. 0021 Component ComponentName too large for this platform. The size of one of the tables associated with the current component exceeds the limits of your platform. The limit for Microsoft Windows is 64 KB. Check the size of your tables and make the appropriate adjustments. 0027 Already initialized. The system has already been initialized; continue. 0028 Application start-up error. This is an ‘umbrella’ message that covers a number of error situations. For designers running their own applications, a generic start-up error message only is displayed, instead of specific Uniface start-up error messages. 0029 Error during embedded SQL generation for entity EntityName . The driver call, open mode 3, failed for the specified entity. This open mode generates embedded SQL files for the entity. 0030 Error during compilation of ModuleName . The compilation command (or a command in the sequence of compilation commands) used for compiling the specified module returned an error status. 0031 Error creating directory DirectoryName . The specified directory could not be created. Check the file or directory protection of the directories under the directory path name. 0032 Not allowed to change when entities have been filled in. You cannot change the DBMS mnemonic after you have retrieved entity names; the DBMS mnemonic determines the directory where the embedded SQL file will be generated. 0033 Specified driver is incapable of embedded SQL generation. The driver for the DBMS that you specified is not capable of embedded generation. Try another DBMS. Unfortunately there is currently no way of knowing which DBMS supports embedded SQL generation. 0034 File name should end in Extension . You can change the file name but not the file extension. 0099 Command line string not acceptable; please check syntax and spelling. Reenter the correct command string. 0102 Not enough occurrences in entity EntityName . You exited the entity frame, but the number of occurrences present is less than the minimum permitted in the Define Component Entity form. Correct the number of occurrences. 0103 Too many occurrences in entity EntityName . This error occurs when you first modify an occurrence and the number of entity occurrences is greater than the maximum specified in the Define Component Entity form. Remove some occurrences, or use ^QUIT to close the edit session. 0104 Field is display-only. You tried to modify a field defined as display-only; this is not allowed. 0105 Not allowed to change primary key field. You cannot modify a primary key field. If you want to modify this key, remove the occurrence and add another occurrence with the modified key, or include a release statement, for example in the store trigger. 0106 Form is display-only. You tried to input or modify data in a component that was run with the display statement instead of the edit statement. 0107 Non-database field FieldName not included in 'order by' list. The field specified is not included in the order by list because it is not stored in the database. To sort records on this field, you must either store the field in the database or write a 3GL routine to order the records. 0111 Field FieldName not available A ProcScript statement references a field that is not included in the field subsetting of the component. Check the ProcScript statements and ensure that the specified field is defined in the Component Editor, or in the modeled entity included in the component. 0112 Failed to open the file for the form USYS:USYSTXT. An error occurred while trying to access the form used to display online help. This might be because the USYSTXT.FRM is not available. Ask your system administrator if the USYSTXT.FRM file exists in the account referenced by the USYS logical. If not, load the component file from the distribution kit. This message can also appear if you use ^HELP while the standard Uniface help form is already on your screen (that is, you are using ^HELP in the help form), because Uniface tries to run the same help form. 0113 Paint Tableau is empty; cannot edit, display or print. Fields are not painted on the Paint Tableau for this component. Correct the component definition. 0114 Failed to start edit on field FieldName b. An attempt was made to start the edit session on a field that Uniface cannot find. Check the edit or display ProcScript statement — it must refer to a field in the layout. 0115 Application FORMAREA is too small for the component window. The dimensions of the FORMAREA frame on the start-up shell Paint Tableau cannot contain the component window. Ensure that the component area frame dimensions are large enough to contain the component windows of the application shell. Note that the minimum size of the component area frame on the application shell Paint Tableau is 3 character cells, otherwise the system components (the message frame and Print form, for example) cannot be activated . 0116 Failed to dispatch to 3GL procedure ProcedureName . Reference was made to a 3GL routine which could not be accessed. Check that any 3GL functions called via the perform statement actually exist, and are spelled correctly. Ensure that the component referencing the 3GL procedure is included in the list of components to be scanned for 3GL and DBMS references on the Define start-up Shell Properties form. Also, if a component is changed (including a 3GL procedure), the application should be compiled and linked again. 0117 Field FieldName not found for $prompt. A ProcScript assignment to $prompt referred to a field that was not included in the layout. Check the $prompt statement and ensure that the field is defined layout. If the field is defined in the layout, ensure that the entity name is also included in the assignment. 0118 More occurrences are not allowed. You entered more occurrences than are allowed, as defined in the entity definition. Limit the number of occurrences, or modify the Number of Occurrences Minimum entry on the Define Component Entity form. 0119 Error on field FIELD: illegal ValRep value. The value you entered in this field does not match any value defined in the ValRep list, or it does not match the range specified in the ValRep list. Enter a value that corresponds to the ValRep value or range of ValRep values. 0120 Error on field FieldName ; value too large. You entered more characters than are allowed in a field (or subfield), as defined in the field syntax template. Use fewer characters, or modify the Maximum Field Length field of the field syntax. 0121 Error on field FieldName ; value too small. The field (or subfield) you exited does not contain the minimum number of characters, as defined in the field syntax. Enter the minimum number of characters for the field or subfield, or modify the Minimum Field Length . 0122 Error on field FieldName ; incorrect checkdigit. The last character in the field does not satisfy the checkdigit modulo, as defined in More Properties of the Define Field Syntax Template form. Supply the correct checkdigit as the last character. 0123 Error on field FieldName ; illegal format for Numeric field. The characters you entered in a data type Numeric field are not permitted for this field. Remove the illegal characters. 0124 Error on field <FieldName> ; illegal format for Date field. The characters you entered in a data type Date field do not match the format for a date field. Correct the entry. 0125 Error on field <FieldName> ; illegal format for Time field. The characters you entered in a data type Time field do not match the format for a time field. Correct the entry. 0126 Error on field FieldName ; illegal syntax format. The characters you entered do not match the entry format defined for the field in the field syntax template. Correct the entry. 0127 Error on field FieldName ; illegal entry format. The characters you entered do not match the entry format defined for the field in the field syntax template. Correct the entry. 0128 Error on field FieldName ; value too large to check. You entered more data than can be checked against the entry format defined in the field syntax template. Remove the entry format definition or make the field smaller. 0129 Error on field FieldName ; value is required. You tried to exit an empty field (or subfield) that is defined as mandatory. Enter a value in the mandatory field. 0130 Error on field FieldName ; too many subfields specified. You exceeded the maximum number of subfields, as defined on the field syntax template. Correct the entry. 0131 Error on field FieldName ; one or more characters not found in character set. Check with your system administrator for permitted character sets. 0132 Error on field FieldName ; illegal numeric. You entered a numeric value for a field, but the data type specified for that field does not include numerics. 0133 Error on field FieldName ; ruler/frames not allowed. This message is self-explanatory. 0134 Error on field FieldName ; italics not allowed. The character attribute definition you entered is not allowed. Note that by default, these characters are only allowed in unifields. Correct the entry. 0135 Error on field FieldName ; underline not allowed. The character attribute definition you entered is not allowed. Note that by default, these characters are only allowed in unifields. 0136 Error on field FieldName ; bold not allowed. The character attribute definition you entered is not allowed. Note that by default, these characters are only allowed in unifields. Correct the entry. 0137 Error on field FieldName ; open/close brackets do not match. A bracket match check is defined for this field on the Define Field Syntax Template form. The check detected that the number of open and closed brackets do not match. Correct the entry. 0138 Error on field FieldName ; illegal format for floating field. The characters you entered do not match data type Float (for a floating decimal point field). Correct the entry. 0139 Entity EnitytName still has restricted links to EntityName2 . You tried to delete a one entity that has a restricted relationship to a many entity. First delete the occurrence or occurrences of the many entity. 0140 Validation failed for field. Use the ProcScript function $dataerrorcontext to find where the error occurred. 0141 No text available in message frame. The message frame does not display data. 0142 Failed to locate online help library. Uniface cannot locate the ulibrary.chm file. Either the file is not present in the expected directory, or an assignment redirection is missing or wrong in your assignment file. 0143 Entry format only allowed for string fields. The entry format of the syntax template shorthand code you specified on the Define Field form is inappropriate for the data type of the field. Check the entry format definition specified on the Define Field Syntax Template form. 0144 Failed to find entry A call statement referred to a ProcScript module that was not found. To help identify the location and entry where the error occurred, a call stack is generated. Define the module or correct the call statement. If you are sure that the global ProcScript module exists and is called correctly, check if the library containing the global ProcScript module was correctly specified in the component or application definition. Note: Uniface searches in SYSTEM_LIBRARY if the global ProcScript module cannot be found in the libraries specified in the component and application definitions. 0145 Entity EntityName not available. A ProcScript statement or function refers to an entity not found on the component structure. Define the entity with the Component Editor. 0146 Component area too small for component geometry. The FORMAREA field frame on the application shell Paint Tableau is smaller than the component window of one of the components addressed by the application shell. Increase the dimensions of the FORMAREA, or modify the definition of the component Width and Depth entries in the Geometry field of the Define Component Properties form. 0147 Validation failed for key. Use the ProcScript function $dataerrorcontext to find where the error occurred. 0148 First occurrence. You used ^PREV_ OCC while editing the first occurrence; a previous occurrence does not exist. 0149 Last occurrence. You used ^NEXT_OCC while editing the last occurrence; a subsequent occurrence does not exist. 0150 Requested number of & and | operators not supported. You included more than one ‘and’ or ‘or’ operators in a field before retrieving. This is not supported. Remove one of the operators. 0151 Query-by-form and u_where clause is too large for internal buffer. Too many fields have been entered in for the Query-By-Form , or in the u_where clause. Reduce the number of selection fields or enter the primary key. 0152 Break frame FrameName not available. The break frame referred to by a printbreak instruction in a ProcScript was not found (for report writing). Check the name you used, and check if the frame definition of the break frame is correct (type should be B). 0153 Validation failed for occurrence. Use the ProcScript function $dataerrorcontext to find where the error occurred. 0155 No component variables found. No component variables were found. To define component variables, click the button next to the Component Variables field in the Define Component Properties form. 0156 No global variables found. This is not an error. No global variables were defined under this library name. 0157 Display format not allowed for this data type. You cannot define a display format for a global or component variable with data type ‘any ($) or string (S)’. 0158 apstart can be used only in the apStart trigger The apstart ProcScript statement is valid only in the apStart trigger of the application shell. 0159 Component variable VariableName not available. The component that you ran referenced a component variable that is not available. Define the component variable for that component and recompile it. 0160 Component | Global variable not available During execution of a ProcScript command, a component variable or global variable was referenced but not found. Ensure that the variable is defined. If it is a global variable, ensure that the library name is correct and that the library is available in the runtime environment. 0163 Indirection failure; field FieldName not available. At run time, Uniface was unable to find a field referred to in ProcScript using an indirect field reference. Check if the field referred to in the operand actually exists, or is correctly named in this context. 0164 Edit instruction only allowed in the exec operation. The edit ProcScript instruction (for specifying the cursor position) is only allowed in the exec operation. Use $prompt if you want to specify that a particular field should be the next editable field. 0165 Query-by-form is too large for internal buffer truncating. Too many fields have been entered in for the Query-By-Form or in the u_where clause. Reduce the number of selection fields or enter the primary key. You are warned that your query will be truncated, consequently more records than you anticipated may be retrieved. Reduce the number of selection fields or enter the primary key. 0168 Divide by zero | Syntax error or illegal operand ( ParameterName ). At least one parameter in your ProcScript statement is incorrect. Uniface generates a call stack to help identify the location of the error. You can also use debug to locate the problem in your code. 0169 Error on field <FieldName> ; illegal format for DateTime field. The characters you entered in a DateTime field do not match the format for a DateTime field. Correct the entry. 0201 Control released; data available as default for new input. You tried to ^CLEAR data that was retrieved from the Repository. The database controls were released with the release statement. Change the primary key and store a new record, or ^CLEAR again to remove all data from the form. 0500 Failed to open the file for the form USYS:USYSPRT. An error occurred while trying to access the USYSPRT.FRM file in the USYS account. This message appears if you try to print the Print form — this is not possible. This error could also occur because the form is not available. Ensure that the system parameters are set correctly. 0502 No matching print job model(s) found. You requested an index on the Choose Print Job Model form, but models that matched the profile you defined were not found. Correct the entry and try again. 0503 Device translation table not available. This message occurs if: You tried to call up a device translation table for a device that does not have a translation table. Check if you entered the correct device name. Remember that printers are usually prefixed by P_. The translation table is not compiled using Uniface 9. Compile the translation table, then try again. 0504 Specify mode (0-7). Enter the number of the device mode that describes paper dimensions and character width. 0515 Nonvalid directory; specify an existing directory. The directory you specified does not exist or could not be accessed; specify an existing directory. 0516 File does not exist; select an existing file. The file you selected does not exist or could not be accessed; specify an existing file. 0517 File already exists; specify a new file. The file you tried to create already exists; specify a new file name. 0600 Load definitions succeeded; Number tables loaded. No warnings or errors were detected while loading the DBMS data repository information. 0601 Load definitions succeeded; see message frame. Warnings were generated while loading the repository information. Refer to the message frame for detailed information. 0602 Load definitions failed; see message frame. Load Definitions detected one or more errors while loading dictionary information. The application model could not be stored. Refer to your message frame for detailed information. 0603 Unsupported data type DataType for field FieldName . The data type found for the column in the repository is not supported by the current database driver. The field is either data type Text or Byte. 0604 Unknown data type DataType for field FieldName . The data type found for the column in the repository is unknown to Load Definitions. 0605 Cannot find index fields for Key of constraint Constraint . Load Definitions was unable to retrieve all column names for the specified primary key or unique constraint. This message indicates an internal error in either the repository or the Load Definitions form. Contact your system administrator. 0606 Indexes not available for table TableName . Indexes for the specified table were not found. Check if you have loaded a view or a table that does not contain indexes. 0607 Table TableName does not have a primary key. Based on the repository information, the Load Definitions form was unable to define a primary key for the specified table. A primary key constraint, unique constraint, or unique index may not have been defined on the table. 0609 Table TableName already exists. You are attempting to load a duplicate definition of the specified object into the repository, or you have selected a table or view that has an identical name but a different owner. 0610 Group GroupName already exists. You are attempting to load a duplicate definition of the specified object into the repository, or you have selected a table or view that has an identical name but a different owner. 0611 Field FieldName already exists. You are attempting to load a duplicate definition of the specified object into the repository, or you have selected a table or view that has an identical name but a different owner. 0612 Index Index of constraint Constraint already exists. You are attempting to load a duplicate definition of the specified object into the repository, or you have selected a table or view that has an identical name but a different owner. 0613 Empty primary key on table TableName already exists. You are attempting to load a duplicate definition of the specified object into the repository, or you have selected a table or view that has an identical name but a different owner. 0614 Index Index on table TableName already exists. You are attempting to load a duplicate definition of the specified object into the repository, or you have selected a table or view that has an identical name but a different owner. 0616 Error when writing table TableName . A serious error occurred while attempting to store the specified object in the repository. A DBMS-specific error message is printed to the message frame by Uniface. 0617 Error when writing group GroupName . A serious error occurred while attempting to store the specified object in the repository. A DBMS-specific error message is printed to the message frame by Uniface. 0618 Error when writing field FieldName . A serious error occurred while attempting to store the specified object in the repository. A DBMS-specific error message is printed to the message frame by Uniface. 0619 Error when writing index Index . A serious error occurred while attempting to store the specified object in the repository. A DBMS-specific error message is printed to the message frame by Uniface. 0620 Error when writing empty primary key on table TableName . A serious error occurred while attempting to store the specified object in the repository. A DBMS-specific error message is printed to the message frame by Uniface. 0621 Error when writing index Index on table TableName . A serious error occurred while attempting to store the specified object in the repository. A DBMS-specific error message is printed to the message frame by Uniface. 0622 To load definitions, you must ^RETRIEVE first. When the Load Definitions form is displayed, enter a profile for the tables you want to load. First you must ^RETRIEVE the tables that match the profile, and then ^ACCEPT to load the definitions. 0623 Table TableName generated warnings; see message frame. Some part of the table definition, for example an index or a field, could not be written to the repository because a duplicate already exists. 0624 Table TableName generated errors. Some part of the table definition, for example an index or a field, could not be written to the repository because a duplicate already exists. 0625 N table(s) handled, N with errors, N with warnings. Information generated after loading database definitions. 0626 Please specify database name. Some DBMSs require the database name on the Load Definitions form. 0627 No tables selected for Load Definitions. Load Definitions could not find a table from which to extract information. 0628 No tables selected. Load Definitions could not find a table from which to extract information. 0629 First unique key becomes the primary key. The primary key for this table has not been defined. The Load Definitions form makes the first unique key the primary key. 0630 Define primary key in application model. DBMS could not find a primary key; define one. 0631 Progress format: %%$1. The data type found for the column in the repository is unknown to Load Definitions . In this case, the default mapping used is S C40. 0632 Table TableName referenced by relationship RelationshipName ( Type ) already exists. Information generated after loading database definitions. 0633 Error when writing table TableName , referenced by relationship RelationshipName ( Type ). A serious error occurred while saving the specified objects to the repository. The corresponding error message from the database system will be printed in the message frame by Uniface. 0634 Load definitions from DB generated N warnings and N errors. Information generated after loading database definitions. 0635 Cannot find foreign key fields for Name of constraint Name . The Load Definitions form cannot retrieve the foreign key fields for the specified primary key or unique constraint. This message indicates an internal error in either the repository or the Load Definitions form. Contact your system administrator. 0636 Cannot find one entity of constraint Name . The Load Definitions form cannot retrieve the one entity for the specified primary key or unique constraint. This message indicates an internal error in the repository or the Load Definitions form. Contact your system administrator. 0637 Load successful: N Tables Loaded, N Warnings - See Message Frame. Load Definitions completed normally. The specified number of supertype entities (tables) were assigned to the application model. During processing, the specified number of warning messages were logged to the message frame. See the message frame for more information. 0638 Load Definitions failed. See message frame. Load Definitions failed to complete normally. Warning and error messages were logged to the message frame. See the message frame for more information. 0639 N tables processed, N warning and 1 error. $STATUS = Value , $RESULT = Value . Uniface could not load an application model due to an internal error in I/O while reading or writing to the repository. Contact your Uniface representative and report the values returned for $status and $result . 0640 Processing table TableName . Displays the name of the table currently loading into the repository. 0641 Processing relationship TableName1 to TableName2 . Displays the name of the tables that form the basis of the relationship currently loading into the repository. 0642 Model updated by version Version of the Database Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that this application model was updated by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for the application model. 0643 Model created by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that this application model was created by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for the application model. 0644 Supertype updated by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that the model definition of this supertype was updated by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for this supertype. 0645 Supertype created by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that the model definition of this supertype was created by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for this supertype. 0646 Subtype updated by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that the model definition of this subtype was updated by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for this subtype. 0647 Subtype created by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that the model definition of this subtype was created by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for this subtype. 0648 Field updated by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that the model definition of this field was updated by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for this field. 0649 Field created by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time . Records that the model definition of this field was created by Load Definitions . This message is added to the repository comments for this field. 0650 Warning: Field FieldName in table TableName . Field type changed from " DataType1 " to "DataType2". The Load Definitions form detected a change to the data type of the field while updating the definition of a field that is already defined. Ensure that any ProcScript associated with this field is valid. 0651 Warning: Field FieldName in table TableName . Field interface changed from " DataType1 " to DataType1 ". Load Definitions changed the interface of the field while updating the definition of a field that was already defined in the application model. Ensure that any ProcScript associated with this field is valid. 0652 Warning: Invalid data type found for field FieldName in table TableName . Load Definitions detected a DBMS data type that could not be mapped to a Uniface data type and interface. The field is mapped to a non-database string with interface C40. 0653 Warning: Invalid data type DataType mapped to existing definition: Type " DataType ", Interface " Interface ". The DBMS data type was mapped to a data type and Interface that already exists for the field specified. Check the field definition and ensure that the existing data type and interface are valid. 0654 Warning: Invalid data type mapped to non-database field: Type "S" Interface "C40". The DBMS data type was mapped to a non-database field of type S with an interface C40. Check the model definition for this field and ensure that a valid data type and interface are defined. 0655 Warning: Primary key for table TableName not defined. Dummy created. Load Definitions could not define a primary key for the specified table. Define the primary key manually with a primary key constraint, unique constraint, unique clustered index, unique index or hashed index. Load the table definition or update the primary key with the Define Fields form. Load Definitions specifies the primary key fields as ‘No primary key defined’. 0656 Warning: Table TableName . DBMS type changed from " DataType1 " to " DataType2 ". Load Definitions has updated the DBMS type for the specified entity. 0657 Warning: Relationship between one entity EntityName1 and many entity EntityName2 cannot be created. Parent table not found. Load Definitions could not find the parent table for the specified relationship. Load the relationship again and ensure that the parent table is included. This forces the relationship to be loaded. 0658 Warning: Relationship between one entity EntityName1 and many entity EntityName2 cannot be created. Child table not found. Load Definitions cannot find the child table for the specified relationship. The relationship cannot be created. Load the relationship again and ensure that the child table is included. This forces the relationship to be loaded. 0659 Warning: Relationship between one entity EntityName1 and many entity % EntityName2 cannot be created. Parent key field(s) FieldNames not defined as primary or candidate keys. Load Definitions cannot find the primary or candidate key that contains fields specified for the parent key of the parent table. This means that the relationship cannot be defined. Check the key defined on the parent table and reload the parent table. This forces the relationship to be loaded. 0660 Warning: Relationship 1 "% EntityName " to many " EntityName " multiple relationships defined between parent and child. New subtype of parent table created " TableName ". Load Definitions detected multiple relationships between a pair of entities. Since a subtype of one of the two entities in the relationship is required to allow both relationships to be defined, Load Definitions automatically created a new subtype of the one entity. Check that the subtypes for the relationship were created correctly. Create a subtype with a more meaningful name and redefine the relationship. 0661 Information: Please review relationship generated by load definitions. Load Definitions is unable to create valid relationship definitions for all relationships defined in the database system tables. If this message is logged in the message frame, you should carefully review the relationships created by Load Definitions . 0662 Created by version Version of the DBMS Load Definitions on Date at Time , to support the one-to-many relationship between TableName1 and TableName2 . The model definition of this subtype was created by Load Definitions to allow multiple relationships to be defined for the tables in question. This message is added to the repository comments for this subtype. 0663 This definition describes: This message is used when logging information on the source object (database table) in the supertype Repository Comments field. 0664 Table: TableName . The database object causing this supertype to be created was a table. This message is added to the repository comments for the supertype. 0665 View: TableName . Records that the database object causing this supertype to be created was a View . This message is added to the repository comments for the supertype. 0666 Owned by: Owner . Records the owner of the database object that caused this supertype to be created. The message is used when the DBMS supports the concept of owners; it is added to the repository Comments for the supertype. 0667 Database: DatabaseName . Records the name of the database which contains the object that caused this supertype to be created. It is used when the DBMS supports the concept of Multiple Logical Databases. This message is added to the repository comments for the supertype. 0668 Loaded from SQL server user-defined data SybaseDataType . Records that the data type and interface for this field were derived from a Sybase SQL Server User-Defined data type. This message is added to the Repository Comments field for the field in question. 0700 Some characters were escaped in:' FileName '; therefore this XML file cannot be imported in Uniface. Self-explanatory. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
